summary: Partially decompiled MSL strtoul support by adding a matching C implementation of the public strtoul wrapper while leaving the hard-to-match internal __strtoul body in assembly. The PR also promoted the ctype classification table and case maps into C as __ctype_map, __lower_map, and __upper_map, replaced assembly references to the old anonymous MSL_AnsiFp_803B8C30 table with __ctype_map, added MSL libc headers needed by the wrapper, and added errno.c/strtoul.c to obj_files.mk. The author explicitly noted that __strtoul differs from other library versions and appears to have a bug involving an unused spaces variable, so the scope was intentionally limited to what was needed to get strtoul matching.

key_files.role: New C implementation of public strtoul wrapper calling __strtoul and handling end pointer, overflow, errno, and negative result adjustment.
New declarations for strtol, strtoul, and internal __strtoul read-proc interface.
Removed old public wrapper assembly, renamed remaining internal routine MSL_StrToUl_80325BD0 to __strtoul, switched ctype table reference to __ctype_map, and removed local sbss errno-like symbol.
Added exported __ctype_map table and renamed lowercase/uppercase maps to __lower_map and __upper_map.
Added MSL ctype flag constants, exported map declarations, and inline classification helpers.
Removed duplicated ctype-like table previously labeled MSL_AnsiFp_803B8C30.
Updated parse_format assembly references from MSL_AnsiFp_803B8C30 to __ctype_map.
Updated assembly references to __ctype_map and changed call from old MSL_StrToUl_80325B24 wrapper label to strtoul.
Added errno declaration and MSL error-code defines including ERANGE.
Added integer limit macros, including ULONG_MAX used by strtoul overflow handling.
Added MSL FILE, file modes/states, console I/O declarations, and related typedefs needed by MSL headers.
Added scan read-proc action enums and string-control structs including __InStrCtrl.
Added src/MSL/errno.c.o and src/MSL/strtoul.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES before asm/MSL/strtoul.s.o.

what_changed: Added src/MSL/strtoul.c with strtoul(const char* str, char** end, int base), constructing an __InStrCtrl, calling __strtoul(base, 0x7FFFFFFF, &__StringRead, &isc, &count, &negative, &overflow), updating *end, returning ULONG_MAX on overflow, setting errno to 0x22, and negating the value for negative input.
Kept the internal conversion parser in assembly but renamed its exported label from MSL_StrToUl_80325BD0 to __strtoul.
Removed the old assembly implementation of the public wrapper MSL_StrToUl_80325B24 from asm/MSL/strtoul.s because the wrapper is now represented in C as strtoul.
Changed assembly call sites and data references to use real library-style names: lb_8001B14C now calls strtoul, and printf/lb/strtoul assembly reference __ctype_map instead of MSL_AnsiFp_803B8C30.
Moved the ctype classification table out of asm/MSL/ansi_fp.s into src/MSL/ctype.c as const unsigned char __ctype_map[256].
Renamed static ctype case-conversion tables to exported const unsigned char __lower_map[256] and __upper_map[256], and updated tolower/toupper to use those names.
Added MSL libc support headers for ctype, errno, limits, ansi_files, scanf, and strtoul declarations.
Added errno.c defining global int errno and added errno/strtoul object files to the text object list.
smart_moves: Scoped the match to the public strtoul wrapper instead of forcing a questionable __strtoul match; the PR body states __strtoul differs from other library versions and appears to have a bug involving unused spaces.
Preserved the unmatched internal parser as assembly while still replacing the wrapper with C, allowing incremental decomp progress without destabilizing behavior.
Gave the ctype data table its canonical MSL symbol name __ctype_map and redirected existing assembly to that symbol, removing duplicate anonymous data from ansi_fp.s.
Introduced the minimum surrounding libc surface needed by strtoul: errno, limits, scanf string-read control structs, and ctype classification helpers.
Used the existing __StringRead/read-proc abstraction instead of inventing a new parser path, matching the internal __strtoul interface visible in assembly.
Handled overflow at the wrapper boundary by setting value to ULONG_MAX and assigning errno = 0x22, matching the assembly wrapper behavior that previously wrote 0x22 and returned -1.
decomp_lessons: For MSL libc routines, the public API wrapper and the internal worker can often be decompiled separately; matching the wrapper may be valuable even when the worker remains stubborn assembly.
When a library function differs from known source versions, avoid overfitting to external source if the local binary shows anomalous behavior.
Canonical data-symbol recovery can unlock multiple files at once: replacing MSL_AnsiFp_803B8C30 with __ctype_map clarified printf, strtoul, and melee lb assembly references.
Promoting shared tables from assembly to C should be paired with assembly reference updates rather than leaving duplicate data labels.
Header work is part of matching work: accurate typedefs, enum values, macro constants, and exported declarations are needed before small libc wrappers can compile and match.
For overflow wrappers, preserve exact observable constants even if the header also defines named values; this PR used errno = 0x22 while errno.h defines ERANGE as 34.
naming_conventions: Use MSL internal double-underscore names for library internals: __strtoul, __ctype_map, __lower_map, __upper_map, __StringRead, __InStrCtrl.
Use public libc names for public API entry points: strtoul, strtol, errno.
MSL header guards in this PR use forms such as _MSL_COMMON_STRTOUL_H, _MSL_COMMON_SCANF_H, _MSL_COMMON_ANSI_FILES_H, MSL_COMMON_SRC_ERRNO_H, and _STD_LIMITS_H.
Ctype flag macros use double-underscore descriptive names: __control_char, __motion_char, __space_char, __punctuation, __digit, __hex_digit, __lower_case, __upper_case.
Scan/read-proc action enums use MSL-style names such as __GetAChar, __UngetAChar, and __TestForError.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly references to MSL_AnsiFp_803B8C30@ha/@l with __ctype_map@ha/@l without changing instruction shape, preserving addresses/relocations while improving symbol accuracy.
Deleted the old public wrapper assembly and added a C object before the remaining asm/MSL/strtoul.s.o in obj_files.mk, enabling the C strtoul symbol while retaining assembly __strtoul.
Renamed an assembly global label for the internal parser to __strtoul instead of continuing to use an auto-generated MSL_StrToUl_80325BD0 name.
Updated external call-site assembly in asm/melee/lb/lb_0198.s from MSL_StrToUl_80325B24 to strtoul once the wrapper became a C symbol.

Moved the ctype table bytes from asm/MSL/ansi_fp.s to a C const array whose initializer encodes the same class-bit pattern using helper macros such as ctrl, motn, spac, punc, digi, hexd, uhex, and lhex.
Removed the local sbss symbol MSL_StrToUl_804D7078 after adding global errno support.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice.

Author rationale from PR body: __strtoul did not match known library versions and appeared to have a bug involving an unused spaces variable, so the submitted change only included what was needed to get strtoul matching.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile or alter __strtoul.
Search for later renames or fixes involving __ctype_map, __lower_map, or __upper_map.
Check whether errno = 0x22 was later replaced with ERANGE or kept for matching.
Search for additional MSL scanf/read-proc decompilation using __InStrCtrl and __StringRead.
Look for follow-up work on MSL headers ansi_files.h, scanf.h, limits.h, and errno.h.
